info.title: Change file mode in global configuration to GroupExecute, GroupRead, UserExecute, UserWrite, UserRead
info.description:
A simple cross platform ACME client (WACS)
Software version 2.3.5.2195 (release, pluggable, dotnet, 64-bit)
Connecting to https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/...
Running as limited user, some options *including autorenewal* disabled
Cronjob not configured yet
Please leave a ★ at https://github.com/simple-acme/simple-acme

options.name: --baseuri
options.description: Address of the ACME server to use. The default endpoint can be modified in

options.name: --test
options.description:
Enables testing behaviours in the program which may help with troubleshooting.
By default this also switches the --baseuri to the ACME test endpoint. The
default endpoint for test mode can be modified in settings.json.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print additional log messages to console for troubleshooting and bug reports.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show information about all available command line options.

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information.

options.name: --config
options.description: Output configuration information in JSON format.

options.name: --renew
options.description:
Renew any certificates that are due. This argument is used by the scheduled
task. Note that it's not possible to change certificate properties and renew
at the same time.

options.name: --force
options.description:
[--renew] Always execute the renewal, disregarding the validity of the current
certificates and the prefered schedule.

options.name: --nocache
options.description: Bypass the cache on certificate requests. Applies to both new requests and

options.name: --register
options.description: Create an ACME service account without creating a certificate.

options.name: --cancel
options.description: Cancel renewal specified by the --friendlyname or --id arguments.

options.name: --revoke
options.description:
Revoke the most recently issued certificate for the renewal specified by the
--friendlyname or --id arguments.

options.name: --list
options.description: List all created renewals in unattended mode.

options.name: --encrypt
options.description: Rewrites all renewal information using current EncryptConfig setting

options.name: --id
options.description:
[--source|--cancel|--renew|--revoke] Id of a new or existing renewal, can be
used to override the default when creating a new renewal or to specify a
specific renewal for other commands.

options.name: --friendlyname
options.description:
[--source|--cancel|--renew|--revoke] Friendly name of a new or existing
renewal, can be used to override the default when creating a new renewal or to
specify a specific renewal for other commands. In the latter case a pattern
might be used. You may use a * for a range of any characters and a
? for any single character. For example: the pattern
example.* will match example.net and example.com
(but not my.example.com) and the pattern ?.example.com will
match a.example.com and b.example.com (but not
www.example.com). Note that multiple patterns can be combined by
comma seperating them.

options.name: --source
options.description:
Specify which source plugin to run, bypassing the main menu and triggering
unattended mode.

options.name: --validation
options.description:
Specify which validation plugin to run. If none is specified, SelfHosting
validation will be chosen as the default.

options.name: --validationmode
options.description: Specify which validation mode to use. HTTP-01 is the default.

options.name: --order
options.description: Specify which order plugin to use. Single is the default.

options.name: --csr
options.description: Specify which CSR plugin to use. RSA is the default.

options.name: --store
options.description:
Specify which store plugin to use. CertificateStore is the default. This may
be a comma-separated list.

options.name: --installation
options.description:
Specify which installation plugins to use (if any). This may be a
comma-separated list.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Specify which certificate profile to use.

options.name: --vaultstore
options.description: Store a new value in the secret vault, or overwrite an existing one.

options.name: --vaultkey
options.description:
Key to target for vault commands. This should be in the format like
vault://json/mysecret.

options.name: --vaultsecret
options.description: Secret to save in the vault.

options.name: --globalvalidation
options.description:
Create a new global validation option to store in the validation.json file.
This will override any other validation options specified at the renewal

options.name: --globalvalidationpriority
options.description: Set priority for the new/updated global validation setting.

options.name: --globalvalidationpattern
options.description:
Set pattern for a new global validation setting, or repeat a previously input
pattern to replace/update the validation settings.

options.name: --closeonfinish
options.description:
[--test] Close the application when complete, which usually does not happen
when test mode is active. Useful to test unattended operation.

options.name: --hidehttps
options.description: Hide sites that have existing https bindings from interactive mode.

options.name: --notaskscheduler
options.description: Do not create (or offer to update) the scheduled task.

options.name: --setuptaskscheduler
options.description: Create or update the scheduled task according to the current settings.
